market_context: U.S. stock futures are mixed with downward pressure as oil prices spike amid U.S.-Iran tensions[2]. The Nasdaq 100 futures are down 0.38%, S&P 500 futures down 0.29%, and Dow Jones futures down 0.21%[2]. However, overnight Asian markets showed the Dow Jones added 0.8% and Nasdaq composite jumped 1.2%[1], suggesting some recovery momentum despite oil concerns.

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures are down 0.29% with oil-driven volatility from U.S.-Iran tensions[2]. While Asian markets showed strength overnight, the persistent futures weakness and geopolitical risk suggest continued pressure through the U.S. session, though confidence is moderate given mixed signals.

Nasdaq 100 futures are down 0.38%, the largest decline among major indices[2], indicating tech sector weakness. Oil price spikes typically pressure growth stocks more heavily than value stocks, and the 0.38% futures decline suggests this weakness will persist into the regular session.

Dow Jones futures are down 0.21%, the smallest decline among major indices[2], but overnight the Dow added 0.8%[1]. This suggests the Dow may show relative resilience, though the modest futures decline and oil headwinds make a flat-to-slightly-down close more likely than a strong recovery.
